failed.title: Minimum interest floor with put right
failed.match: PASS if the compromise includes a minimum interest floor (a specified percentage below which a non-funding member cannot be diluted) coupled with a put right allowing the non-funding member to sell its interest rather than being trapped in a de minimis position. FAIL if there is no minimum floor or no put right at the floor.

failed.title: Non-compete product scope includes pipeline products meeting R&D-spend threshold
failed.match: PASS if the product scope includes pipeline products that meet a defined R&D-spend threshold (not just products already sold at exit). FAIL if pipeline products are excluded entirely or included without any qualifying threshold.

failed.title: Non-compete carve-outs for Kastor's legacy European chemical business and raw-material sales
failed.match: PASS if the non-compete includes carve-outs protecting Kastor's legacy European chemical business and/or raw-material sales, per kastor-position-summary.docx. FAIL if no carve-outs are provided for Kastor's broader business activities outside the JV scope.

failed.title: Non-compete geography defaults to North America (U.S., Canada, Mexico)
failed.match: PASS if the default geographic scope is North America (U.S., Canada, Mexico), consistent with Meridian's position per mkpoly-jva-v3-clean.docx. FAIL if the default geography is worldwide, or limited only to territories where JV had active sales (Kastor's position).

failed.title: Non-compete includes product-by-product geographic narrowing mechanism
failed.match: PASS if the provision includes a product-by-product narrowing mechanism that limits geography for product lines sold in fewer than all three North American countries (a concession to Kastor). FAIL if the North America default applies uniformly to all products without narrowing.

failed.title: Appraiser selection mechanism: each party selects one, those two select a third
failed.match: PASS if the appraisal process includes a selection mechanism where, if the parties cannot agree on one appraiser, each selects one and those two select a third. FAIL if the mechanism is absent or uses a different process.

failed.title: Baseball-style bracketing for appraisal
failed.match: PASS if the appraisal process includes baseball-style bracketing (the appraiser's final value cannot be outside the range of the two parties' submitted values). FAIL if the appraiser has unconstrained discretion or no bracketing mechanism is specified.

failed.title: Buy-sell includes shoot-out/Russian roulette mechanic
failed.match: PASS if the buy-sell provision includes a shoot-out (Russian roulette) mechanic where the triggering party names a price and the receiving party may elect to buy or sell at that price, providing bilateral price discipline. FAIL if no such mechanic is included.

failed.title: Sublicensing to controlled affiliates freely permitted within territory
failed.match: PASS if sublicenses to controlled affiliates are permitted freely within each party's licensed territory. FAIL if affiliate sublicensing requires consent or is prohibited.

failed.title: Third-party sublicensing requires other member's consent (not unreasonably withheld)
failed.match: PASS if sublicenses to third parties (non-affiliates) require the other member's consent, with such consent not to be unreasonably withheld. FAIL if third-party sublicensing is freely permitted or absolutely prohibited without a consent mechanism.

failed.title: Transitional cross-license for contributed IP in existing JV products at royalty rate
failed.match: PASS if the compromise includes a transitional cross-license for contributed IP embedded in existing JV products, at a royalty rate, for a defined wind-down period following dissolution or exit. FAIL if there is no transitional license for contributed IP (Meridian's position of automatic reversion with no transition) or if the transition license is royalty-free.

Section 4.03 — Capital Calls and Dilution

[CONTESTED PROVISION — Competing tracked-changes markup from both sides'

to dilution; (iii) dilution cap.]

(a) Capital Calls. The Management Committee may determine from time to
time that additional Capital Contributions are required to fund the
business, operations, capital expenditures, working capital needs, or
other requirements of the Company. Upon such determination, the
Management Committee shall deliver a written Capital Call notice to each
Member specifying (i) the aggregate amount of additional Capital
Contributions required, (ii) each Member's proportionate share of such
amount (based on each Member's then-current Percentage Interest), (iii)
the purpose for which such additional Capital Contributions are
required, and (iv) the date by which such Capital Contributions must be
made, which date shall be not less than thirty (30) days after delivery
of the Capital Call notice.

(b) Obligation to Fund. Each Member shall fund its proportionate share
of a Capital Call within thirty (30) days after receipt of the Capital
Call notice (the "Funding Deadline"). Each Member's proportionate share
of any Capital Call shall be determined by reference to such Member's
then-current Percentage Interest. Capital Contributions made in response
to a Capital Call shall be credited to the contributing Member's Capital
Account in accordance with Section 4.02.

(c) Failure to Fund; Cure Period.

If a Member (the "Defaulting Member" or "Non-Contributing Member") fails
to fund its proportionate share of a Capital Call within ninety (90)
days of the Capital Call notice (the "Cure Period"), the non-defaulting
Member (the "Contributing Member") may, at its sole election, exercise
any one or more of the remedies set forth in Section 4.03(d); provided,
further, that during the Cure Period, the Contributing Member shall have
the option (but not the obligation) to extend to the Defaulting Member a
loan in an amount equal to the Defaulting Member's unfunded portion of
the Capital Call, at a per annum interest rate equal to the Default
Rate, such loan to be evidenced by a promissory note and secured by a
first-priority security interest in the Defaulting Member's membership
interest in the Company. If the Contributing Member elects to extend
such loan and the Defaulting Member accepts the loan and repays it in
full (including all accrued and unpaid interest) within one hundred
eighty (180) days of the date of such loan, no Dilution Event shall
occur with respect to such Capital Call.

as an alternative to dilution. Ninety-day cure period provides adequate

===== SECTION 5.01 — MANAGEMENT COMMITTEE (lines 762-941) =====
Section 5.01 — Management Committee Composition, Voting, and Deadlock

[CONTESTED PROVISION — Competing tracked-changes markup from both sides'
counsel. Meridian counsel: Whitfield Ames & Trotter LLP (B. Falk).
Kastor counsel: Rennert Krieger Solano LLP (T. Escalante). Unresolved
issues: (i) number of Managers and allocation of appointment rights;
(ii) voting thresholds; (iii) deadlock resolution mechanism.]

(a) Establishment. The business and affairs of the Company shall be
managed by a management committee (the "Management Committee"). The
Management Committee shall have full, exclusive, and complete authority,
power, and discretion to manage and control the business and affairs of
the Company and to take all actions and make all decisions on behalf of
the Company, except as otherwise expressly provided in this Agreement or
as required by the Act. Each Manager shall be a "manager" of the Company
within the meaning of the Act. The Members shall not have any authority
to act on behalf of or bind the Company, except as expressly delegated
by the Management Committee or as otherwise provided in this Agreement.

(b) Composition.

The Management Committee shall consist of six (6) members (each, a
"Manager"): three (3) Managers appointed by Meridian (the "Meridian
Managers") and three (3) Managers appointed by Kastor (the "Kastor
Managers"). The initial Managers appointed by Meridian shall be Elaine
Cordero, James M. Adeyemi, and Patricia Yoon. The initial Managers
appointed by Kastor shall be Dr. Henrik Laufer, Claudia Rüegg, and
Fabian Grünwald.

Kastor redline comment (Rennert Krieger Solano — T. Escalante): "Equal
board representation is essential and reflects the near-parity of
capital contributions ($42.9M vs. $43.8M). Kastor is in fact the larger
capital contributor on a dollar basis and should not accept a minority
position on the governing body. Three Managers for each Member ensures
balanced governance and protects Kastor's investment."

Meridian redline comment (Whitfield Ames & Trotter — B. Falk): "The
55/45 equity split warrants proportional board control. Meridian holds
the majority equity position and is contributing the core proprietary
coating technology that forms the basis of the JV's product portfolio. A
3-2 Management Committee reflects Meridian's majority equity position
and is consistent with standard majority-holder governance rights. The
near-parity of dollar contributions is a function of the IP valuation,
not a basis for equal governance."

Each Member may remove and replace any Manager appointed by it at any
time, with or without cause, by delivering written notice to the other
Member and to the Company. Each Manager shall serve until such Manager's
successor is appointed and qualified, or until such Manager's earlier
death, resignation, incapacity, or removal. Vacancies in any Manager
position shall be filled by the Member entitled to appoint such Manager.
No Manager shall be entitled to any compensation from the Company for
service on the Management Committee, but each Manager shall be entitled
to reimbursement of reasonable, documented out-of-pocket expenses
incurred in connection with attendance at Management Committee meetings
or other Company business.

(c) Voting.

Each Manager shall have one (1) vote. All actions of the Management
Committee shall require the affirmative vote of a majority of the
Managers then in office, provided, however, that actions with respect to
Reserved Matters (as defined in Section 5.02) shall require the
affirmative vote of at least five (5) of the six (6) Managers then in
office (i.e., a supermajority requiring the affirmative vote of at least
one (1) Kastor-appointed Manager and at least two (2) Meridian-appointed
Managers).

Kastor redline comment (Rennert Krieger Solano — T. Escalante): "A CEO
casting vote effectively gives Meridian unilateral control over all
Management Committee decisions, rendering the Management Committee a
mere formality. This is unacceptable in a joint venture structure. The
supermajority requirement for Reserved Matters ensures that both Members
have meaningful governance participation on the most significant
decisions affecting the Company."

Meridian redline comment (Whitfield Ames & Trotter — B. Falk):
"Meridian, as the majority equity holder, requires the ability to break
deadlocks to prevent operational paralysis. The CEO casting vote is a
standard mechanism and is limited to tie votes that cannot be resolved
through negotiation. Kastor's proposed supermajority for Reserved

"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4):
        print("Usage: generate_from_md.py <input.md> <output.docx> [template.docx]", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    template = Path(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) == 4 else None
    generate(Path(sys.argv[1]), Path(sys.argv[2]), template)

===== CAPITAL CALLS CLAUSE (sample) =====
Section 4.03 — Capital Calls and Dilution

(a) Capital Calls. The Management Committee may determine from time to
time that additional Capital Contributions are required to fund the
business, operations, capital expenditures, working capital needs, or
other requirements of the Company. Upon such determination, the
Management Committee shall deliver a written Capital Call notice to each
Member specifying (i) the aggregate amount of additional Capital
Contributions required, (ii) each Member’s proportionate share of such
amount (based on each Member’s then-current Percentage Interest), (iii)
the purpose for which such additional Capital Contributions are
required, and (iv) the date by which such Capital Contributions must be
made, which date shall be not less than fifteen (15) business days after
delivery of the Capital Call notice.

(b) Obligation to Fund. Each Member shall fund its proportionate share
of a Capital Call within sixty (60) calendar days after receipt of the
Capital Call notice (the “Cure Period”). Each Member’s proportionate
share of any Capital Call shall be determined by reference to such
Member’s then-current Percentage Interest. Capital Contributions made in
response to a Capital Call shall be credited to the contributing
Member’s Capital Account in accordance with Section 4.02.

(c) Penalty Interest. If a Member (the “Defaulting Member”) fails to
fund its proportionate share of a Capital Call within fifteen (15)
calendar days after receipt of the Capital Call notice, penalty interest
shall accrue automatically on the unfunded amount at a rate per annum
equal to the greater of (i) the Secured Overnight Financing Rate (SOFR)
plus five hundred (500) basis points, or (ii) ten percent (10%) (the
“Penalty Rate”). Penalty interest shall be payable to the Company for
the benefit of the non-defaulting Member (the “Contributing Member”)
upon funding or upon dilution, as applicable. If the Defaulting Member
funds its full proportionate share plus all accrued penalty interest
within the Cure Period, no Dilution Event shall occur.

(d) Euro Funding Accommodation. To the extent that Kastor’s funding
obligations are funded from euro-denominated accounts, Kastor may
satisfy its funding obligation in euros at the European Central Bank
reference rate quoted on the date of the Capital Call notice, with
conversion to U.S. dollars handled by the Company’s treasury at the
Company’s cost. This accommodation is available to either Member funding
from non-USD accounts.

(e) Loan Alternative. During the Cure Period, the Defaulting Member may
elect, by written notice to the Contributing Member and the Company, to
have the Contributing Member advance the Defaulting Member’s unfunded
share of the Capital Call as a member loan (a “Bridge Loan”). If a
Bridge Loan is elected:

(i) The Bridge Loan shall bear interest at a rate per annum equal to
    SOFR plus four hundred (400) basis points, compounding quarterly.

(ii) The Defaulting Member shall grant to the Contributing Member a
     first-priority security interest in its entire membership interest
     in the Company as collateral for the Bridge Loan, and the parties
     shall execute and file UCC financing statements promptly upon
     disbursement.

(iii) The Bridge Loan shall be repayable in full (principal plus accrued
      interest) within twelve (12) months of disbursement, from the
      Defaulting Member’s share of Distributions and any other amounts
      otherwise distributable to it.

(iv) If the Defaulting Member fails to repay the Bridge Loan in full
     within the twelve (12)-month repayment period, the outstanding
     balance (principal plus accrued interest) shall automatically
     convert to equity, resulting in dilution of the Defaulting Member’s
     Percentage Interest calculated in accordance with Section 4.03(f)
     and subject to the Annual Dilution Cap.

(v) The Bridge Loan alternative shall be available to any Member no more
    than once in any twenty-four (24)-month period. Any subsequent
    failure to fund within such twenty-four (24)-month period shall
    result in dilution without the option of a Bridge Loan.

===== IP LICENSE-BACK CLAUSE (sample) =====
Section 12.05 — IP License-Back on Dissolution or Exit

(a) Reversion of Contributed IP. Upon dissolution of the Company or the
completion of a buy-sell transaction under Article X, each Member’s
Contributed IP shall revert to the contributing Member. The Company (or
the purchasing Member, as applicable) shall execute and deliver to each
contributing Member such instruments of assignment, transfer, and
conveyance as are reasonably necessary to effect such reversion.

(b) License to Co-Developed IP. Upon reversion of Contributed IP
pursuant to Section 12.05(a), each Member shall receive a non-exclusive,
royalty-free, perpetual, irrevocable, worldwide license to use the
Co-Developed IP in all fields of use, subject to the territorial
sublicensing restrictions set forth in Section 12.05(c):

(i) Meridian License. Meridian may use the Co-Developed IP in North
    America and in any other territory in which Meridian or its
    Affiliates generated at least Five Hundred Thousand Dollars
    ($500,000) in revenue from specialty polymer coatings in the
    twelve (12) months preceding the applicable exit or dissolution
    event.

(ii) Kastor License. Kastor may use the Co-Developed IP in all
     territories outside North America.

Neither license is exclusive. Neither party’s use of Co-Developed IP in
its respective territory is restricted by the other party’s use.

(c) Sublicensing Rights and Restrictions.

(i) Territorial Sublicensing. Each Member may sublicense Co-Developed IP
    to third parties within its respective territory (Meridian: North
    America and its active-operations territories; Kastor: territories
    outside North America), subject to the restrictions set forth in
    this Section 12.05(c).

(ii) Restricted Competitors Prohibition. Neither Member shall sublicense
     Co-Developed IP to any Person listed on Schedule 12.05 (Restricted
     Competitors), regardless of the territory in which the sublicense
     would be exercised, without the prior written consent of the other
     Member, which consent may be withheld in such other Member’s sole
     and absolute discretion. This prohibition applies reciprocally to
     both Members and to all Restricted Competitors listed on Schedule
     12.05, whether identified by Meridian (Part A) or by Kastor (Part
     B).

(iii) Revenue-Based Negative Covenant. In addition to the named
      Restricted Competitors on Schedule 12.05, neither Member shall
      sublicense Co-Developed IP to any Person that derives more than
      twenty-five percent (25%) of its consolidated annual revenue from
      the manufacture, sale, or distribution of specialty polymer
      coatings in the other Member’s territory (i.e., for purposes of
      Kastor’s sublicensing, in North America; for purposes of
      Meridian’s sublicensing, outside North America). This covenant
      operates as a dynamic anti-circumvention mechanism that captures
      entities not named on Schedule 12.05, including new market
      entrants, entities that restructure to avoid the named-entity
      list, and foreign companies with material coatings operations in
      the other Member’s territory.

(iv) Audit Right. Each Member shall have the right, upon reasonable
     prior written notice and at its own expense, to audit the other
     Member’s sublicensing records no more than once per calendar year
     to verify compliance with the territorial restrictions, the
     Restricted Competitors prohibition, and the revenue-based negative
     covenant set forth in this Section 12.05(c). The audited Member
     shall reasonably cooperate with such audit and shall make relevant
     sublicensing records available. If an audit reveals a material
     breach of the sublicensing restrictions, the audited Member shall
     bear the cost of the audit and shall promptly cure the breach.
